zh.lifeSummary: 當Christian Douglas於大約1395年出生在蘇格蘭金洛-爾時，她的父親Sir William Douglas of Lochleven14歲，母親Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay7歲。她和David Wemyss of Kincaldrum, of Methil and Wemyss至少有2個兒子和6個女兒。她去世於1465年在蘇格蘭費伏維梅斯享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: Christian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

variants.name: Christianne
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

en.lifeSummary: When Christian Douglas was born about 1395, in Kinross-shire, Scotland, her father, Sir William Douglas of Lochleven, was 14 and her mother, Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay, was 7. She had at least 2 sons and 6 daughters with David Wemyss of Kincaldrum, of Methil and Wemyss. She died in 1465, in Wemyss, Fife, Scotland, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Christian Douglas est née environ 1395, à Kinross-shire, Écosse, son père, Sir William Douglas of Lochleven, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay, avait 7 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 6 filles avec David Wemyss of Kincaldrum, of Methil and Wemyss. Elle est décédée en 1465, à Wemyss, Fife, Écosse, à l’âge de 71 ans.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Christian Douglas родилась около 1395 г. в местности кинросс-шир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir William Douglas of Lochleven было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay, 7. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей от David Wemyss of Kincaldrum, of Methil and Wemyss. Она умерла в 1465 г. в местности вемисс, фиф, Шотландия в возрасте 71 год.
